title: Join External Race-State Signals: Safety Car, VSC, Weather, Race Control
summary: Public F1 data sources expose exactly the exogenous shocks that shift pit-stop timing: weather, track status, safety car/VSC, and race-control messages.
rationale: Public F1 data sources expose exactly the exogenous shocks that shift pit-stop timing: weather, track status, safety car/VSC, and race-control messages. Those state changes are largely absent from the provided columns but are directly strategy-relevant.
implementation_hint: For each Race-Year-Lap, join OpenF1 or FastF1 race-control, track-status, and weather data. Add features like active SC/VSC flag, laps since status change, rainfall/track-temp trend, and whether a stop under neutralization would reduce pit-loss. Test a dry-only baseline versus enriched wet/neutralized subsets separately.
expected_effect: Most likely gain is on rare but high-information regimes where current rowwise tyre features are weak, especially weather transitions and neutralized races.
risk: Temporal alignment is the trap: lap timestamps, session times, and race-control event times must be aligned conservatively to avoid using messages posted after the decision lap.
